user_message: Help me build pipeline hygiene for Litware Inc. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 11:23 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 15:10 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 09:27 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-03-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is pipeline hygiene.

[00:06:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:20:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:31:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-B trap (Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-B failure mode (Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-B -- Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language
